event: Mary with Child Surrounded by a Garland of Flowers
symbols: flowers surrounding the Madonna — creation offering its beauty to the Mother of God
roses — Mary as the Mystical Rose, love and the blood of martyrdom
tulips — the fleeting beauty of earthly life contrasted with eternal grace
butterflies — resurrection and the transformation of the soul
stone niche with sculpture — the enduring truth of the Incarnation framed by the beauty of creation
description: A dark, richly detailed painting showing a stone cartouche or architectural niche at center containing a small sculptural relief of the Virgin Mary with the Christ child. Surrounding the niche, three lush garlands of flowers are arranged — one crowning the top and two flanking the sides below. The flowers include roses, tulips, peonies, carnations, and other blooms in pinks, reds, whites, yellows, and blues. Small white butterflies flutter among the flowers. Ivy and other greenery trail from the arrangements.
theological_description: Seghers's garland painting belongs to a rich Flemish tradition of surrounding sacred images with floral wreaths — a visual theology of creation offering its finest beauty to the Creator. The Virgin and Child at the center are the theological focus, while the abundant flowers represent the created world praising its Maker. The Psalmist declares: 'The heavens declare the glory of God, and the sky above proclaims his handiwork' (Psalm 19:1). The flowers themselves echo Jesus's teaching: 'Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin, yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these' (Matthew 6:28-29). The roses recall the Song of Solomon's imagery applied to Mary: 'I am a rose of Sharon' (Song of Solomon 2:1). The butterflies symbolize resurrection — 'the mortal puts on immortality' (1 Corinthians 15:54). The dark background against which the flowers blaze with color suggests that divine beauty shines brightest against the darkness of the fallen world. This is creation as liturgy — all things declaring the glory of the one born of the Virgin.

scripture_refs.reason: The heavens declaring God's glory — creation's beauty offering praise around the sacred image
The lilies of the field surpassing Solomon — the abundant floral beauty surrounding the Madonna
The rose of Sharon — roses surrounding Mary as the Mystical Rose
Mortality putting on immortality — the butterflies as symbols of resurrection
